svg.title: Spoovel v0 — minimal field engineering schematic
svg.desc: Side-view schematic of the Spoovel v0: a wheeled frame with a sloped chute, bag-holding fork, hand winch or rope pull, and body-height working position. Labels show parts list, load paths, stress points, field repair notes, and what not to over-engineer yet. Commons field tool — no batteries, no proprietary parts.
svg.text: Spoovel v0 — minimal field tool
Fox's Anvil: Open Resilience Tools · commons engineering schematic · no rights reserved
Build it badly. Improve it. Share what you changed.
ground
sack / bag
load →
A · wheel / axle
bicycle wheel or barrow wheel
replace first if terrain is rough
B · main frame
2×4 lumber or welded angle iron
C · sloped chute
plywood, sheet metal, or tarp
slope 20–35° · line with plastic
D · bag-mouth holder
rebar hooks or bolt-on forks
⚠ STRESS POINT: bag weight
E · hand winch / crank
rope + pulley or bike brake cable
optional in v0 — rope pull works
F · push handles
conduit, pipe, or hardwood
Parts list — v0 minimal build
Frame 2×4 lumber or 1.5" angle iron, ~3m total · bolts or lag screws throughout
Wheels 2× bicycle wheel or wheelbarrow wheel · 5/8" axle bolt · locking nut each side
Chute 3/4" plywood (600×900mm) or sheet metal scrap · 2× carriage bolts to frame
Bag holder 2× rebar stakes 300mm or bolt-on U-brackets · cross-bar from conduit or pipe
Winch Hand winch (optional) or 10m rope + 2 pulleys · rated 200kg min if winch used
Handles 1.5" conduit or 2×2 hardwood · ~900mm each · secured with U-bolts
Hardware M10/M12 bolts · flat washers · lock nuts · 2× cotter pins for axle
Load path
Material weight → chute surface → chute-to-frame bolts [⚠ primary stress] → frame rails
→ bag holder crossbar [⚠ secondary stress] → upright posts → axle → wheels → ground
Filled bag weight: sandbag ~25kg · soil ~30kg · design frame for 50kg concentrated load
Stress points and expected failures
⚠ Chute-to-frame joint Highest torque point. Use 2× M12 bolts with large washers. Check every session.
⚠ Bag holder crossbar Bends under bag weight. Use pipe not lumber. Reinforce with gusset if needed.
⚠ Wheel axle Shear risk if overloaded laterally. Lock nut + cotter pin. Replace before rust sets.
⚠ Rope/winch line First thing to fail under repeated load. Inspect before every use. Always carry spare.
Field repair notes
Replace first: rope · axle cotter pin · chute bolts — carry spares of each on the tool
Emergency fix: ratchet strap or baling wire can replace most failed connections temporarily
Do not over-engineer yet: tie-off station · measuring counter · CodeTape mount · motorization
